content:
Got to love developers that think they are making things easier for us.

Lets first try and work out which is which.

So lets check a couple of things are correct before deleting anything. Right click your account name and select subscribe. Are all three folders shown (I expect they will be.) Open account settings. From that same right click menu select settings Under the "Server Settings" pane check the folder that is named as the correct one for trash. Change it is necessary. While we are cleaning up. Your image shows two sent folders. So under copies and folders, check the folder name actually used for sent mail by selecting other and setting it manually to "sent mail" on the account name (that is what Google use). I think what is happening is Thunderbird is using sent and Google is using sent mail and both are being displayed as "sent".

So after the change you should still have two sent folders, but one will no longer have the green icon and should be deleted.

Note that you can right click on a folder name and select properties from the menu. This will show the "real" name of the folder not what Thunderbird is using to display to you. It is this substitution that evoked my initial comment.
creator: MattAuSupport

The following steps are very important to keep a chance to retrieve your Inbox messages: • in Thunderbird go to menu Help > Troubleshooting Information > Profile Folder > Open Folder, then quit Thunderbird • your profile folder "xxxxxxxx.default-release" (or similar name) is now displayed in your Windows Explorer: copy the profile folder to some repertory, e.g. your desktop • in the copied profile folder open the ImapMail folder; it may contain one or several subfolders of the type "imap.xxx.xx" • if you have only one account open the "imap.xxx.xx" subfolder and take a screen shot of its contents; take care that file sizes appear in the screen shot or at least note the size of the INBOX file.

@Judy, I've seen that you have opened a new thread here https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1584960 in which you say that your problematic email account uses an IMAP server. Please continue here in your old thread, otherwise communication gets chaotic.

Go to the menu Help > Troubleshooting Information > Profile Folder > Open Folder, then quit Thunderbird. Now in Windows Explorer (or file manager?) you'll see your Thunderbird profile folder named somewhat like "xxxxxxxx.default-release" (or similar). Make a copy of this profile folder and keep it on the desktop of your PC. Open the copy of the profile folder, go to ImapMail subfolder and open it. In ImapMail probably you'll see two account folders of the type "imap.xxx.xx". Identify the "imap.xxx.xx" account folder which corresponds to your problematic account and take a screen shots of its contents, showing folders, files, date and file size.

content:
Open your profile folder ☰ > Help > troubleshooting information Click on the open Folder button beside the Profile folder entry in the table. Close down Thunderbird. It can not be running for the next step

Open the mail folder in the file manager/explorer window that opened from the last step Locate the "smart mailboxes" folder in the mail folder and delete it (nothing else)

Restart Thunderbird. That will force a full rebuild of everything related to the smart mailboxes (unified views original name) and will hopefully cure your issue permanently.

content:
I suppose that you open your messages in new tabs in the tab bar to read them without closing the tabs later. After a while there will be so many messages opened in individual tabs that the tab bar cannot display all of them. Since each new tab opens on the right end of the tab bar, the Inbox mail tab will disappear from the left side of the tab bar.

a) Use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + 1 to switch to the mail tab with your Inbox. or b) if you don't remember the shortcut, click on the down arrow ∨ symbol on the right end of the tab bar and select "Inbox" in the list of open tabs (image)

content:
Para diagnosticar problemas en Thunderbird, prueba una de las siguientes soluciones:

  • Reinicia Thunderbird con los complementos desactivados (Thunderbird en Modo Seguro). En el menú de Ayuda, haz clic en "Modo de resolución de problemas ..". Si Thunderbird funciona bien, hay algún complemento o tema que interfiere en el funcionamiento normal de Thunderbird. Tendrás que volver a activar todos los complementos hasta que localices dónde está el problema.
  • Reinicia el sistema operativo en Modo a prueba de fallos. De esta forma se cargará solo lo necesario para que pueda arrancar el ordenador y se activará la conexión a Internet. Para obtener instrucciones sobre cómo reiniciar tu sistema operativo en modo seguro, haz clic en él: Windows 8, Si reiniciando el sistema operativo en modo seguro se ha resuelto el problema, el que causa los problemas es algún otro software de tu equipo. Algunas de esos softwares pueden ser (aunque no son los únicos): AV scanning, virus/malware, descargas de fondo (por ejemplo, actualizaciones de programas), etc.

content: Thunderbird is just an email client. It does not check for mailbox full, and does not have any block limit, nor does it verify sender. All of that is done by your email provider and, since Thunderbird is your agent, Thunderbird dutifully delivers those messages to you. I suggest you check with your email provider on these errors. The serder verify error could possibly be within thunderbird if the account is not set up properly. On that, you might check the email address of the account and the email address of the SMTP server settings pane to see if they match and if the full email address is there.
creator: davidsk
